_comment: Per-project atelier configuration. Created by /atelier:setup-project. The operator may edit this file; atelier never overwrites it once it exists. To reset to defaults, delete the file and re-run /atelier:setup-project.

prSize._comment: Per-PR size budget enforced by atelier-pr-size-check (M7.1.F27). A PR is OVERSIZE when BOTH limits are exceeded after exempting auto-generated / test / migration files. Tune these as your project's reviewability tolerance evolves; very small repos may want stricter values, monorepos may want larger ones.

taskDecomposer._comment: Autonomous task decomposition (M4.24.a defines the Epic / sub-task format; M4.24.b adds the agent that auto-rewrites large tasks into epics before implementer runs). Set `enabled: false` to turn off the automatic pass — the operator can still invoke /atelier:slice-task <id> manually. The orchestrator's heuristic for `oversize-likely` only fires when this flag is true.

taskConcurrency._comment: How many tasks /atelier:next-task may have in flight at once for this repo (M7.1.F66). Each task runs in its own worktree cut from origin/<base>, so parallel tasks are isolated; the claim registry is the set of open `task/*` PRs on origin, not a single IN_PROGRESS.md slot. Raise `max` to parallelise non-colliding work — across workspace members it is always safe; within one repo, tasks with overlapping `scope:` may collide at merge time (the auto-merge gate resolves or escalates). Absent field is treated as `1`.

ciWait._comment: Bounded wait for CI checks to reach a terminal state before the auto-merge gate (#23). After `reviewer` approves, the orchestrator waits up to `maxWaitSeconds` for any `IN_PROGRESS`/`QUEUED` checks to complete before invoking the auto-merge skill. `FAILURE`/`CANCELLED`/`TIMED_OUT`/`STARTUP_FAILURE` stops the chain immediately without merging. A timeout yields `held: CI still running after <maxWaitSeconds>s` and does not consume the 6-attempt retry budget. Repos with no CI (empty statusCheckRollup) are not waited on. Absent block → built-in defaults.

reviewFix._comment: Bounded automated review-fix loop (#24). When `enabled: true`, a `reviewer` → `request-changes` verdict automatically re-dispatches `implementer` (and `tester` when coverage is implicated) on the same worktree/branch, re-pushes, and re-runs `reviewer` — up to `maxCycles` times — before escalating to the operator. Set `enabled: false` to revert to today's surface-and-stop terminal: findings are surfaced immediately and the PR is left open for manual follow-up. Only code-addressable findings (correctness / test coverage / code quality / security) drive the loop; structural findings (scope alignment, oversize, missing dependency justification, pending human comments) route to their existing handlers or escalate directly without consuming a cycle. Absent block → built-in defaults (`enabled: true`, `maxCycles: 2`).

babysitPrs._comment: Per-pass bounds for /atelier:babysit-prs (#25). `maxPrsPerPass` caps how many task/* PRs are dispatched to the orchestrator in a single pass — PRs beyond the cap are deferred to the next pass. This is about per-pass scope, not a poll interval or loop count; the recurring cadence is delegated to the Claude Code built-in /loop command. Absent block → built-in defaults.

_deliverableLanguage_comment: Language atelier writes repo deliverables in — commit messages, PR titles/descriptions, code comments, and generated docs/artifacts (.plan/<id>.md, drafted CLAUDE.md, ROADMAP/IN_PROGRESS/HISTORY entries) — regardless of the project's own content language or the operator's chat language (M7.1.F73). Set to any language name (e.g. "Spanish", "French"). Absent field is treated as "English". Does not affect operator-facing chat or the project's pre-existing content.

semanticRiskJudge._comment: Layer-3 second gate above auto-mode (M2.9). When `enabled: true`, the semantic-risk-judge PreToolUse hook asks Haiku 4.5 to judge Bash commands that touch a high-risk surface (lockfile, container build, CI/CD, package manifest, deploy/infra — catalogued in $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/patterns/semantic-risk-judge.json) and escalates risky ones to an operator `ask`. Off by default: the static deny-list + auto-mode already cover most cases, and each judged command spends a short Haiku call. Fail-open — if Haiku is unavailable the command is allowed and a degraded line is written to .task-log/hook-decisions.jsonl. Only commands on the high-risk surface ever reach the model.

decisionPolicy._comment: Per-project policy for the decision broker (M4.26). Each catalog category maps to one of three values: "auto" (an evaluator agent decides per-case — Haiku/Sonnet/Opus depending on the category's riskLevel), "ask" (atelier asks the operator like it does today), or a fixed option id (e.g. "fix-first" — always pick that option without thinking). `default` covers categories not listed individually. Generated by `/atelier:setup-project` interactive prompts (run `/atelier:set-policy` to revise without re-running setup). The full catalog of categories lives at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/agents/decision-broker/catalog.json — operators do NOT edit the catalog. Fresh-install default is `ask` for everything; M4.26.a base ships dormant and integration wires through this block when M4.26.c lands.
